subtitle: A secluded, rip-torn cove at the tip of Point Nepean, long hidden inside restricted military land — the beach where a sitting Australian Prime Minister swam out on a December morning in 1967 and was never seen again.
spike_statement: The secluded, dangerous cove where Australia's sitting Prime Minister Harold Holt swam out on 17 December 1967 and vanished — his body never recovered.

levers.photo_tone: a wild, empty, cold-water cove backed by scrub-covered dunes and limestone, rough grey Bass Strait surf, flat overcast Victorian light, not a person in sight — a beach that is closed
